686	// dispatchReviewRun resolves how the review invocation should be executed.
687	// Resolution order matches dispatchAgentRun:
688	//  1. r.Runner (test injection seam) — used directly via Runner.Run.
689	//  2. r.Service (pre-built service) — used via RunViaServiceWith.
690	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(ProjectRoot)
691	//     and dispatch via RunViaServiceWith.
692	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runOpts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
693		if r.Runner != nil {
694			return r.Runner.Run(runOpts)
695		}
696		svc := r.Service
697		if svc == nil {
698			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(r.ProjectRoot)
699			if err != nil {
700				return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: build agent service: %w", err)
701			}
702			svc = built
703		}
704		return RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, r.ProjectRoot, runOpts)
705	}

708	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
709	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
710	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
711	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
712		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
713		defer cancel()
714		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
715		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
716		if err != nil {
717			return "", err
718		}
719		return string(out), nil
720	}

417		DefaultHarness = "codex"
418		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
419		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
420		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
421		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
422		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
423		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
424		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
425		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
426		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
427		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
428		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
429		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours

433	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
434	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
435	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
436	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
437	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
438		if configured == "" {
439			configured = DefaultLogDir
440		}
441		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
442			return configured
443		}
444		if projectRoot == "" {
445			return configured
446		}
447		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
448	}

304			Sections: sections,
305		}
306	}

308	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
309	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
310	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
311	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
312	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
313	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
314	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
315		if len(files) == 0 {
316			return originalDiff
317		}
318		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
319			if f.Path == "" {
320				return 3
321			}
322			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
323				return 0
324			}
325			for _, r := range refs {
326				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
327					return 1
328				}
329			}
330			return 2
331		}
332		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
333		copy(ordered, files)
334		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
335	
336		var b2 strings.Builder
337		for _, f := range ordered {
338			body := f.Body
339			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
340				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
341			}
342			b2.WriteString(body)
343		}
344		return b2.String()
345	}
346	
347	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
348	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
349	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
350	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
351	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
352		var b strings.Builder
353		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
354		b.WriteString(f.Path)
355		b.WriteString(" b/")
356		b.WriteString(f.Path)
357		b.WriteString("\n")
358		if f.Stat != "" {
359			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
360			b.WriteString("\n")
361		}
362		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
363			b.WriteString(h)
364			b.WriteString("\n")
365		}
366		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
367		return b.String()
368	}

370	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
371	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
372	// exact symbolic resolution).
373	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
374		if text == "" || p == "" {
375			return false
376		}
377		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
378			return true
379		}
380		base := path.Base(p)
381		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
382	}
383	
384	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
385	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
386	// documents.
387	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
388		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
389			return false
390		}
391		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
392		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
393			return true
394		}
395		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
396			return true
397		}
398		return false
399	}

401	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
402	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
403		var buf bytes.Buffer
404		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
405			"&", "&",
406			"<", "<",
407			">", ">",
408		).Replace(s))
409		return buf.String()
410	}

412	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
413	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
414	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
415	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
416		ProjectRoot string
417		BeadStore   BeadReader
418		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
419		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
420		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
421		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
422		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
423		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
424		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
425		Runner AgentRunner
426		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
427		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
428		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.
429		Harness string
430		Model   string
431		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
432		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
433		// applies.
434		Caps evidence.Caps
435	}

437	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
438	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev, implHarness, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
439		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
440		if err != nil {
441			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
442		}
443	
444		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
445		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
446		if err != nil {
447			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
448		}
449	
450		// Resolve governing document references.
451		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
452	
453		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
454		iter := 1
455	
456		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
457		caps := r.Caps
458		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
459			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
460		}
461		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
462		prompt := built.Prompt
463		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
464		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
465		if err != nil {
466			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
467		}
468		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
469			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
470		}
471	
472		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
473		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
474		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
475		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
476		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
477		if built.Overflow {
478			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
479			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
480				Sections:    built.Sections,
481				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
482				OutputBytes: 0,
483				Harness:     r.Harness,
484				Model:       r.Model,
485			}
486			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
487				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
488				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
489				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
490				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
491				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
492				BaseRev:         baseRev,
493				ResultRev:       resultRev,
494				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
495				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
496				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
497			}
498			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
499				Harness:          r.Harness,
500				Model:            r.Model,
501				BaseRev:          baseRev,
502				ResultRev:        resultRev,
503				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
504				BeadID:           beadID,
505				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
506				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
507			}, reviewArtifactResult{
508				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
509				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
510				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
511			})
512			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
513				evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow, len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
514		}
515	
516		// Resolve reviewer harness and model.
517		reviewHarness := r.Harness
518		if reviewHarness == "" {
519			if implHarness != "" {
520				reviewHarness = implHarness
521			} else {
522				reviewHarness = "claude" // default reviewer harness
523			}
524		}
525		reviewModel := r.Model
526		if reviewModel == "" {
527			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
528		}
529	
530		start := time.Now()
531		runOpts := RunOptions{
532			Context: ctx,
533			Harness: reviewHarness,
534			Model:   reviewModel,
535			Prompt:  prompt,
536			WorkDir: r.ProjectRoot,
537		}
538		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runOpts)
